articles.title: Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush
articles.description: Rutendo Nyeve-Bulawayo Bureau A RECORD number of local and international tourists are visiting the Victoria Falls as the waterfall reaches its peak, defying fears that numbers could drop due to... The post Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush appeared first on herald .

articles.title: Zimbabwe Cricket confirms Aussie tour
articles.description: ZimpapersSports Hub ZIMBABWE Cricket have confirmed that former world champions Australia will tour the country for a three-match One-Day International series in September this year. All fixtures will be played... The post Zimbabwe Cricket confirms Aussie tour appeared first on herald .

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: UK government blocks Kanye West from London music fest
more.description: The UK government on Tuesday blocked US rapper Kanye West from visiting the country to headline a music festival due to his past antisemitic outbursts, forcing organisers to cancel the event. The rapper had submitted an application to travel to Britain on Monday, but it was refused because his presence would not be conducive to […]

more.title: Revenue falls but ZSE Holdings returns to profitability
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Stock Exchange Holdings posted a modest profit for the year ended December 31, 2025, despite a sharp decline in revenue, demonstrating resilience in a difficult operating environment.Zimbabwe economic experts According to the group’s audited financial results, revenue fell by 15 percent to US$6,38 million from US$7,55 million in 2024, reflecting reduced activity across […]

more.title: Wealthy Chinese are transforming Zimbabwe’s housing market
more.description: An influx of wealthy Chinese immigrants has prompted real estate agencies to hire Mandarin speakers as demand for high-end homes drives double-digit price increases in Harare, Bloomberg reported. Many buyers pay in cash, skirting exchange controls and limiting government tax revenue. China’s economic footprint in Zimbabwe has surged over the past five years: Beijing dominates […]

more.title: Chamisa’s ‘goodnight’ post ignites social media frenzy
more.description: Former opposition leader Nelson Chamisa has stirred debate online after posting a relaxed photo of himself smiling, captioned simply “goodnight.” Within minutes, the post drew a flood of reactions, with supporters and critics weighing in almost equally. While some praised the former Citizens Coalition for Change leader, others used the moment to question his silence […]

more.title: Zimbabwe tightens import rules to boost local industry
more.description: Zimbabwe’s newly introduced import restrictions under Statutory Instrument (SI) 59 of 2026 are expected to strengthen local manufacturing and protect domestic industries, according to analysis by Lucent Consultancy. The regulations, issued under the Control of Goods Act, are designed to curb foreign currency outflows, improve product quality standards, and promote local production by tightening controls […]

more.title: US$4m bank heist mastermind denied bail
more.description: The Bulawayo High Court has denied bail to a man accused of masterminding a daring US$4 million robbery at an Ecobank branch, in a case that has drawn widespread public attention. The suspect, Bhekani Milo, is facing charges linked to a high-stakes heist that unfolded on October 3, 2024, at Ecobank’s Parkade branch in Bulawayo. […]

more.title: RBZ Rolls Out New Zig Banknotes in Phased Launch
more.description: [263Chat] The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe has begun introducing a new series of ZiG banknotes, as authorities seek to strengthen confidence in the country's currency.

more.title: New ZiG notes begin circulating through ATMs
more.description: Automated Teller Machines (ATMs) in Harare have started dispensing newly upgraded Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) banknotes following their official rollout on Tuesday. Customers at several banks, including a branch in Borrowdale, were among the first to access the new ZiG10 and ZiG20 denominations, marking the initial phase of the updated currency’s circulation through ATM systems. The […]

more.title: Global shares mostly higher ahead of Trump’s deadline for Iran to reopen oil route
more.description: TOKYO — Global shares mostly rose in cautious trading Tuesday, as oil prices continued to surge ahead of a deadline that U.S. President Donald Trump set for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping traffic or risk its power plants and bridges being bombed. France’s CAC 40 jumped 1.3% to 8,066.18 in […]

more.title: Anti-Mnangagwa protesters denied bail
more.description: HARARE – A group of 98 suspected protestors accused of taking to the streets in Harare on March 31 to demand President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s exit were Thursday denied bail by Harare magistrate Isheanesu Matova. In his ruling, Matova concurred with prosecutors that the group would commit the same offence if granted bail. “It is in […]

more.title: Kuwadzana 6 Shooting: ‘Peacemaker’ Dodges Bullets After Stepping Between Man And Bouncers
more.description: Kuwadzana 6 Shooting: Man Fires Gun At ‘Peacemaker’ For Stepping Between Him And Bouncers A Harare man has appeared in court after allegedly firing a gun at a “peacemaker” who tried to calm a dispute between him and bar bouncers at Kuwadzana 6 Shops. Simbarashe Mhute, 37, of Westgate, appeared before the Harare Magistrates’ Court [...] The post Kuwadzana 6 Shooting: ‘Peacemaker’ Dodges Bullets After Stepping Between Man And Bouncers appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: “My heart was racing”: a breathtaking encounter with an elephant in Zimbabwe
more.description: Untouched surroundings, sleeping under the stars and cocktails with hippos – a safari adventure in Zimbabwe is an experience you won’t forget

more.title: Bus from Zimbabwe intercepted after legally entering SA
more.description: Source: Bus from Zimbabwe intercepted after legally entering SA Border Management Authority (BMA) Commissioner Michael Masiapato has provided clarity on how a cross-border bus, initially cleared at the port of entry of Limpopo’s Beitbridge border post, later ended up transporting additional 32 illegal immigrants. The vehicle, en route from Zimbabwe to Cape Town, was stopped [...] The post Bus from Zimbabwe intercepted after legally entering SA appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: CAB3 undermines accountability: Human Rights commission
more.description: Source: CAB3 undermines accountability: Human Rights commission -Newsday Zimbabwe The Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC) has raised constitutional and democratic concerns over key provisions in the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3), warning that the changes risk undermining accountability, public participation and the rule of law. Addressing the media on the Commission’s analysis of [...] The post CAB3 undermines accountability: Human Rights commission appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Government widens second-hand car import ban
more.description: Source: Government widens second-hand car import ban – herald Farirai Machivenyika, machivenyikafari@gmail.com THE Government has expanded the ban on the importation of second hand vehicles aged 10 years or older from the date of manufacture, extending the restriction to include vehicles designed to carry 10 or more passengers, among other specified categories. The prohibition is contained [...] The post Government widens second-hand car import ban appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .
